content: Hypothyroidism treatment: levothyroxine (synthetic T4) replacement, dosed by weight (~1.6 mcg/kg/day), adjusted based on TSH every 6-8 weeks until stable. Take on empty stomach, separate from calcium/iron supplements. Target TSH 0.5-4.5 mIU/L (individualized; lower targets in pregnancy, thyroid cancer). Hyperthyroidism treatment options: (1) Antithyroid drugs: methimazole first-line (except first trimester pregnancy - use PTU), typical duration 12-18 months for Graves'; (2) Radioactive iodine (I-131): definitive therapy, contraindicated in pregnancy/breastfeeding, often leads to hypothyroidism; (3) Thyroidectomy: for large goiters, suspicion of malignancy, patient preference. Beta-blockers (propranolol, atenolol) for symptom control. Thyroid storm: medical emergency requiring ICU, antithyroid drugs, iodine, steroids, beta-blockers.
